assertions.text: Driver saw the danger well ahead (sight/recognition of hazard before braking).
Driver braked hard (heavy braking) for about four to five seconds before the collision.
Initial speed before braking was about 60 km/h.
Braking occurred before the collision (braked for 4–5 seconds, then collision happened).
There was a collision (one impact with another object/vehicle).
Despite heavy braking from about 60 km/h, the vehicle still struck the other object/vehicle.
After the collision, the vehicle came to a stop.
The braking was described as heavy; collision was not avoided (implies non-trivial impact) but no explicit description of major severity given.

verdicts.evidence: The claim is about driver perception; no direct sensor shows sight/recognition. IMU and GPS show braking began before impact ([sustained accel t_start 5.61 to 6.9](T4)) but that does not prove 'saw the danger well ahead'. Therefore this is unverifiable.

Telemetry shows sustained braking-like deceleration on device accel_x from [t=5.61 to t=6.9 with mean magnitude 0.55 g](T4) and on accel_y from [t=5.93 to t=6.46 with mean magnitude 0.51 g](T4), and a GPS speed drop from [60.0 km/h at t~4–6 to 35.0 km/h by t~8 (a drop of 25.0 km/h between t=5.0 and t=8.0)](T4). The claimant said braking for about [four to five seconds]; GPS speed samples show speed declining from [63.0 km/h at t=0] to [52.0 km/h at t=7] and then to [35.0 km/h at t=8] and reaching [0.0 km/h at t=11–16], but the sustained deceleration window detected began around [t=5.61](T4) lasting ~[1.3 s] not the full 4–5 s; however another sustained window spans [t=7.51 to t=8.01](T4). Overall the presence of sustained deceleration leading up to impact is supported by IMU ([sustained accel windows](T4)) and GPS speed drop ([from 60.0 to 35.0 km/h between t=5.0 and t=8.0](T4)).

GPS speed samples show the vehicle near [63.0 km/h at t=0], [62.0 km/h at t=1], [61.0 km/h at t=2], and [60.0 km/h at t=4–6] with a first_value of [63.0 km/h] and max of [63.0 km/h] in the window ([gps_speed samples and stats](T2) and (T3)). This supports an initial speed of about [60 km/h].

Detect_events shows sustained deceleration windows starting at [t=5.61 to 6.9] and impact spikes around [t=6.74–7.77] including a peak at [t=7.44] and speed drop detections from [t=5.0 to t=8.0], indicating braking preceded the impact ([sustained accel t_start 5.61](T4) and impact spikes at [t=7.41 and 7.44](T4)).

Multiple impact spikes are detected on accelerometer axes with peaks at [t=6.75, t=7.41, and t=7.77] and magnitudes up to [3.95 g] on accel_x/accel_y, indicating a collision event occurred ([impact spikes list](T4)) and accel stats report peak_abs_dev [3.953 g at t=7.44](T3).

GPS speed shows a drop from about [60.0 km/h at t~5] to [35.0 km/h by t=8.0] despite sustained deceleration windows detected from around [t=5.61 to 6.9] and again [t=7.51 to 8.01], and an impact spike occurred during that interval (impact at [t=7.41–7.44]) showing the vehicle struck another object despite heavy braking ([speed_drop from 60.0 to 35.0 km/h between t=5.0 and t=8.0](T4) and [impact spikes at t=7.41–7.44](T4)).

GPS speed samples show the last nonzero speed at [t=10.0 was 10.0 km/h and t=11.0 was 0.0 km/h], and compute_stats reports last_value [0.0 km/h at t=16.0] with change [−63.0 km/h], indicating the vehicle came to rest after the event ([gps_speed points](T2) and [gps_speed stats last_value 0.0 at t=16.0](T3)).

Sensors record sustained braking magnitudes (mean ~[0.55 g] on accel_x between t=5.61–6.9 and mean ~[0.51 g] on accel_y between t=5.93–6.46) and impact spikes with peak magnitude up to [3.95 g at t=7.44], supporting that braking was heavy and the collision was non-trivial though no explicit injury/severity label is present ([sustained accel values and impact peak](T4) and [peak_abs_dev 3.953 g at t=7.44](T3)).

result.rationale: Telemetry shows the vehicle was traveling ~[63.0 km/h] initially, underwent sustained deceleration windows starting around [t=5.61], experienced impact spikes peaking at [t=7.44] with up to [3.95 g], and came to rest by [t=16 with gps 0.0 km/h], which aligns with the narrative that heavy braking from ~[60 km/h] preceded a collision and the vehicle stopped (sensors cannot verify driver sighting).

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_z.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
